description: Install and configure Vitest + React Testing Library + jsdom for the Next.js project. Set up test infrastructure including vitest config, test setup file, and package.json scripts.
steps: Run 'source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh && npm install' to install existing dependencies first
Install test dependencies: vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom, @testing-library/user-event, jsdom, @vitejs/plugin-react as devDependencies

Create vitest.config.ts at project root that: uses @vitejs/plugin-react, sets test environment to jsdom, configures path alias '@/*' to resolve to './*' (matching tsconfig.json paths), includes setup file, and sets globals:true
Create a test setup file (e.g., vitest.setup.ts) that imports '@testing-library/jest-dom' for extended matchers
Add 'test' script to package.json: 'vitest run' and optionally 'test:watch' script: 'vitest'
Create a minimal smoke test (e.g., __tests__/smoke.test.ts) that asserts 1+1===2 to verify the setup works
Run 'source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh && npx vitest run' to confirm the test infrastructure works end-to-end
acceptance_criteria: Running 'npx vitest run' succeeds with at least one passing test
vitest.config.ts exists with jsdom environment, React plugin, and path alias configured
package.json has vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom, @testing-library/user-event, jsdom in devDependencies
